message:
fix(engine): implement member-expression assignment lowering in IR1 pipeline

The compound/simple assignment lowering previously only handled
identifier LHS targets (e.g. x = 1, x += 1). Member expression
targets (e.g. obj.prop = 1, arr[i] += 1) fell through to the Nop
placeholder branch, silently discarding the assignment's side effect.

Changes:
- Move the right-hand-side lowering inside each LHS branch so that
  evaluation order matches JS semantics: for identifiers, evaluate
  RHS then store; for member expressions, evaluate object first,
  then RHS, then emit SetProperty
- Add explicit Expression::Member branch: lower the object expression,
  lower the RHS expression, extract the property key (from Identifier
  or StringLiteral, defaulting to "unknown" for computed expressions),
  and emit Ir1Op::SetProperty { key }
- Non-identifier, non-member LHS still falls through to Nop (covers
  destructuring and other complex patterns not yet supported)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
